county_metadata.parent_county: Original county (Three Lower Counties on Delaware)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kent County formed 1680 as one of the Three Lower Counties on Delaware under William Penn's charter. County seat is Dover, which has also served as Delaware's state capital since 1777. The county was originally called St. Jones County (after the St. Jones River). Predominantly agricultural with the capital city as its urban center. Dover Air Force Base (est. 1941) has had significant impact on the county's population and demographics.
county_metadata.research_tips: Delaware Public Archives (DPA) in Dover is the primary repository for Kent County and all Delaware records, holding original deeds from the 1680s, probate files, orphans court records, and county levy court minutes. The Kent County Recorder of Deeds office in Dover maintains land records from 1680 to the present. The state capital has been in Dover since 1777, so colonial and state government records are concentrated here. Kent County's Quaker (Friends) meetings in Camden and Duck Creek produced baptism, marriage, and burial records from the early 1700s -- check Swarthmore College Friends Historical Library for originals and microfilm. Methodist records are also strong in Kent County from the late 1700s, as the peninsula was a center of early Methodism (Barratt's Chapel, 1780, is the 'Cradle of Methodism in America'). For African American research, Kent County had a significant free Black population before the Civil War -- check manumission records at DPA, free negro registers, and African Methodist Episcopal church records. Tax assessments from 1726 at DPA are excellent for tracking family movements. FamilySearch has digitized many Kent County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: DPA microfilm and digitized images for Kent County deed books from 1680; Kent County Recorder of Deeds for recent records

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Deed Books -- Delaware Public Archives / Recorder of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Public Archives, Dover / Kent County Recorder of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Kent County from 1680; DPA holds microfilm copies and some digitized images; Recorder of Deeds has originals and recent records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival; deed books are substantially complete from 1680 with no major gaps

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Probate and Orphans Court Records -- DPA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, inventories, administrations, orphans court proceedings, and guardianship records for Kent County; orphans court handled estate matters and guardianships of minors
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1680; orphans court records are particularly detailed for Kent County

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Marriage Records -- DPA / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage bonds and licenses from 1744; earlier marriages found in church records; state marriage registration began 1847; FamilySearch has indexed Delaware marriages

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1790-1950 for Kent County; Delaware was enumerated in every federal census; 1800 and 1810 schedules are complete

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Tax Assessment Lists -- DPA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment lists for Kent County hundreds (subdivisions) from 1726; list heads of household, property, livestock, and enslaved persons; essential for pre-census research
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1726 with some gaps in early years

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Church Records -- Methodist, Quaker, Anglican
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Public Archives / Swarthmore College Friends Historical Library / Local congregations
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Methodist records from Barratt's Chapel (1780, 'Cradle of Methodism') and other circuits; Quaker (Friends) meeting records for Camden and Duck Creek from early 1700s; Anglican/Episcopal parish records. Baptisms, marriages, burials

source_profiles.source_name: Kent County Enslaved Persons and Free Negro Records -- DPA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Manumission records, free negro bonds, slave sale records, estate inventories listing enslaved persons, assessments of enslaved people in tax records, and AME church records documenting African American families in Kent County

county_metadata.parent_county: Original county (Three Lower Counties on Delaware)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: New Castle County formed 1673 as the earliest of the Three Lower Counties on Delaware, originally established under Dutch administration. County seat is Wilmington, Delaware's largest city and principal industrial center. The town of New Castle served as colonial capital until 1777. Home to DuPont company headquarters and the Brandywine Valley industrial corridor. The most urban and populous of Delaware's three counties, with strong ties to Philadelphia and southeastern Pennsylvania.
county_metadata.research_tips: Delaware Public Archives (DPA) and the New Castle County Recorder of Deeds office hold the primary records. New Castle County has the oldest European settlement records in the Delaware Valley, dating to the New Sweden colony (Fort Christina, 1638) and Dutch administration (1655-1664). Holy Trinity (Old Swedes) Church in Wilmington has baptism and burial records from 1698 -- one of the oldest continuous church records in America. The Historical Society of Delaware in Wilmington holds extensive manuscript collections, family papers, business records, and Wilmington city directories from the 1840s. For immigration research, Wilmington was a significant port -- check ship passenger lists and naturalization records at NARA (Philadelphia) and DPA. The Hagley Museum and Library holds DuPont family and company records plus Brandywine Valley industrial records essential for tracing workers and families in the powder mills and textile factories. Quaker (Friends) meetings in Wilmington, Newark, and Hockessin have records from the 1680s at Swarthmore College. For African American research, Wilmington was a major Underground Railroad station -- check Thomas Garrett's papers and the Delaware Historical Society's African American collections. Court of Chancery records at DPA are unusually rich for equity cases involving estates, guardianships, and property disputes.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: New Castle County Recorder of Deeds online index and images for recent deeds; DPA microfilm for historical deed books from 1673

source_profiles.source_name: New Castle County Deed Books -- DPA / Recorder of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Public Archives, Dover / New Castle County Recorder of Deeds, Wilmington
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Land records for New Castle County from 1673; Recorder of Deeds has online index and images for recent records; DPA holds microfilm of historical deed books; early records include Dutch and Swedish land transactions
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival; continuous series from 1673 is one of the oldest in the US

source_profiles.source_name: New Castle County Wills and Estate Records -- DPA / Register of Wills
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Public Archives, Dover / New Castle County Register of Wills, Wilmington
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, inventories, administrations, and estate accounts for New Castle County; Register of Wills office has recent records; DPA has historical will books on microfilm; includes Dutch-period testaments
source_profiles.survival_notes: Substantially complete from the 1670s; among the earliest probate records in the mid-Atlantic

source_profiles.source_name: New Castle County Naturalization Records -- DPA / NARA Philadelphia
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Public Archives, Dover / NARA Mid-Atlantic Region, Philadelphia
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Naturalization petitions and declarations of intention filed in New Castle County courts; includes Superior Court and US District Court records; DPA holds county court naturalizations; NARA Philadelphia has federal court records

source_profiles.source_name: New Castle County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1790-1950 for New Castle County including Wilmington; Delaware was enumerated in every federal census; Wilmington's industrial population makes census records especially useful for immigrant family research

source_profiles.source_name: New Castle County Church Records -- Swedish (Old Swedes), Quaker, Anglican
source_profiles.repository: Historical Society of Delaware / Holy Trinity (Old Swedes) Church / Swarthmore College Friends Historical Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Holy Trinity (Old Swedes) Church baptism and burial records from 1698 (among the oldest continuous church records in America); Quaker meeting records for Wilmington, Newark, and Hockessin from 1680s; Anglican/Episcopal parish records including Immanuel Church, New Castle (1689)

source_profiles.source_name: Wilmington and New Castle County Historic Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Public Archives / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wilmington newspapers from 1785 including Delaware Gazette, Every Evening, and Wilmington Morning News; legal notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, estate settlements, and shipping news

source_profiles.source_name: New Castle County Court Records -- DPA / Court of Chancery
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Court of Chancery equity cases (unique to Delaware -- the Chancellor's court handles estates, guardianships, property disputes); Superior Court criminal and civil records; Justice of the Peace records; Dutch and early English court proceedings from the 1670s
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival; Chancery records are particularly well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: New Castle County Military Records -- DPA / NARA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Revolutionary War service records (Delaware Line, Continental Army), War of 1812, Civil War Union service (DE remained in Union), WWI/WWII draft registrations; DPA holds state militia and adjutant general records; Fort Delaware on Pea Patch Island was a Civil War prison -- prisoner records at NARA

county_metadata.parent_county: Original county (Three Lower Counties on Delaware)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sussex County formed 1680 as one of the Three Lower Counties on Delaware under William Penn's charter. County seat is Georgetown. Originally called Deale County (or Deal). The largest county by area and the most rural, with agriculture (poultry, corn, soybeans), fishing, and timber as historic economic drivers. Lewes, at the mouth of Delaware Bay, was the site of early Dutch and English settlement. Sussex County had the highest concentration of enslaved persons in Delaware and a large free African American population by the antebellum period.
county_metadata.research_tips: Delaware Public Archives (DPA) and the Sussex County Recorder of Deeds in Georgetown hold the primary records. Sussex County is the largest Delaware county by area and was historically agricultural with significant timber and fishing industries. Early settlement was English (Lewes area, 1631 Dutch attempted settlement, then English from 1664). Church records are particularly important -- Anglican/Episcopal parishes (St. Peter's, Lewes, from 1681) and Presbyterian congregations have records at DPA and the Delaware Historical Society. Sussex County had the highest enslaved population in Delaware and the largest free Black community by 1860 -- manumission records, free negro bonds, and African American church records at DPA are essential for researching these families. The Nanticoke Indian community in the Indian River area has records at DPA and the Nanticoke Indian Museum. Orphans court records at DPA are especially valuable for Sussex County, documenting guardianships, apprenticeships (including African American children), and estate distributions. Land records go back to the 1680s and reflect the large plantation-style agriculture of the Delmarva Peninsula. The Sussex County Genealogical Society maintains indexes and transcriptions of local records. FamilySearch has microfilmed Sussex County deed books, probate records, and orphans court proceedings.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: DPA microfilm for Sussex County deed books from 1680; Sussex County Recorder of Deeds in Georgetown for recent records

source_profiles.source_name: Sussex County Deed Books -- DPA / Recorder of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Public Archives, Dover / Sussex County Recorder of Deeds, Georgetown
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Land records for Sussex County from 1680; DPA holds microfilm of historical deed books; Recorder of Deeds office in Georgetown has originals and recent records; early deeds reflect the large agricultural landholdings of the Delmarva Peninsula
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival; deed books are complete from 1680

source_profiles.source_name: Sussex County Probate and Orphans Court Records -- DPA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, inventories, administrations, and orphans court proceedings for Sussex County; orphans court records are especially valuable for documenting guardianships and apprenticeships (including African American children after manumission)
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete from 1680; orphans court apprenticeship records are uniquely detailed

source_profiles.source_name: Sussex County Cemetery Records -- Delaware Genealogical Society / Find A Grave
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Sussex County churches and family burial grounds; many rural cemeteries in agricultural areas have been documented by the Delaware Genealogical Society; Find A Grave has growing coverage

source_profiles.source_name: Sussex County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1790-1950 for Sussex County; slave schedules (1850, 1860) document enslaved persons in Sussex County's plantation economy; agricultural schedules provide farm data

source_profiles.source_name: Sussex County Enslaved Persons, Manumission, and Free Negro Records -- DPA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Sussex County had Delaware's highest enslaved population. Manumission records documenting emancipation, free negro bonds (required for free Black persons to post security), slave sale bills, estate inventories listing enslaved persons by name, and tax records enumerating enslaved persons by slaveholder
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival; manumission records are particularly complete for Sussex County

source_profiles.source_name: Sussex County Death Records -- DPA / Delaware Division of Public Health
source_profiles.repository: Delaware Public Archives, Dover / Delaware Division of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Death records for Sussex County from 1881 (state registration); earlier deaths found in church burial records, cemetery records, and newspaper obituaries; DPA has historical death records; modern records at Division of Public Health

source_profiles.source_name: Sussex County Land Warrants and Patents -- DPA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Colonial and early state land warrants, surveys, and patents for Sussex County; Penn's commissioners granted land in Sussex County from the 1680s; survey records document metes and bounds of original tracts

source_profiles.source_name: Sussex County Tax Assessment Lists -- DPA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment lists for Sussex County hundreds from 1726; list property owners, acreage, livestock, and enslaved persons; hundreds (subdivisions) include Broadkiln, Cedar Creek, Dagsboro, Indian River, Lewes and Rehoboth, Nanticoke, and Northwest Fork
source_profiles.survival_notes: Survival varies by hundred; generally good from the mid-1700s forward
